entry.title: Object no longer valid at de.hybris.platform.jalo.Item$CachedGetter.tryToCompute
entry.content:
I'm getting this exception during order creation process. I could see the details of the PK value which thrown in exception. but when I query for the same PK in DB, it's showing no results found. 

How come if the entry is not there in DB but showing when I search in PK analyzer?

Exception Stacktrace:

Entity not found ( pk = 14835026264110 name = 'de.hybris.platform.persistence.core_OrderEntry' type code = '46' db table = 'orderentries'): object no longer valid at de.hybris.platform.jalo.Item$CachedGetter.tryToCompute(Item.java:773) at de.hybris.platform.jalo.Item$CachedGetter.get(Item.java:759) at de.hybris.platform.jalo.ExtensibleItem.getProperty(ExtensibleItem.java:343) at de.hybris.platform.jalo.order.GeneratedAbstractOrderEntry.getDiscountValuesInternal(GeneratedAbstractOrderEntry.java:259) at jdk.internal.reflect.GeneratedMethodAccessor1377.invoke

entry.title: Re: New Beginnings: Introducing the Beyond SAP Cloud channel on YouTube
entry.content: Very much excited for upcoming videos.

entry.title: Re: Uploading Data to S/4HANA On-Premise from BTP HANA Cloud DB Using SDI Integration
entry.content: Hi, are you really trying to insert data to a virtual table? As far as I understand, you want to do data replication (and not virtualisation). Then, shouldn't you habe a target table on the target system? And mustn't you rather insert the data to that target table than to a virtual table?

entry.title: Archive the previous years' bank statements up to 30-Jun-2023
entry.content: How can I archive bank statements from previous years, up to June 30, 2023, in our production ECC system? I am a beginner and need a step-by-step guide through the entire process.

entry.title: Re: SAPUI5: Date picker produces error "duplicate id" in...
entry.content: Update: fixed by SAP with SAPUI version 1.133.0 and retrofitted into 1.120.26.

entry.title: Re: problem with conversion to S/4HANA 2023 phase XPRAS_A...
entry.content: works for me. THANKS

entry.title: Re: Custom Business Configuration
entry.content: @Ram_peddireddiAll ADT wizards have in common that existing objects are not overwritten. You can either delete the generated objects manually and run the ADT wizard again. Or you add or remove the fields manually to or from the generated RAP BO.

entry.title: Stopping blank reports whilst bursting
entry.content:
Hi, we have a story which shows our sellers quotes for the day, we have this set up using data access controls and a publication in burst mode. Is there anyway to only send a report when there is data for them? Currently everyone on the seller team receives a report even if blank.
Any advice appreciated, thank you.

entry.title: The Power of Joule for Developers, ABAP AI Capabilities
entry.content:
There are only a few moments like this during a professional lifetime. This is huge, and I’m certain many of you will remember this moment years later – it is game-changing.

We’re thrilled to announce today Joule for developers, ABAP AI capabilities. The wait is over. ABAP AI is here for you. As I announced earlier, a real treasure.

The AI capabilities for ABAP are designed to enhance developer productivity by delivering new generative AI-powered assistance for frequent developer tasks. The ABAP AI capabilities utilize a purpose-built large language model (LLM) trained on millions of lines of ABAP code to deliver the most precise, contextual results for ABAP workloads. We’re offering these ABAP AI capabilities free to all customers using SAP BTP ABAP environment or SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition, until September 15, 2025.  

 

Enhancing Developer Efficiency  

At SAP, we remain steadfast in our commitment to delivering unparalleled value and improving the lives of developers around the world. This steadfastness is evident by our continued investment in AI features across the SAP portfolio, including Joule for developers, AI capabilities. At SAP TechEd in October last year, we announced how we’re bringing SAP Build and ABAP Cloud closer to each other and introducing new AI assistive capabilities. 

These new AI capabilities for ABAP Cloud include:

  • AI Chat Assistance: Developers can receive real-time assistance via Joule in ADT by asking questions about ABAP programming or related processes. Developers have access to real-time chat assistance, embedded in the ABAP for Eclipse Integrated Development Environment (IDE). 
  • Code Explanation: Developers can retrieve detailed explanations of selected ABAP or ABAP Core Data Services (ABAP CDS) code to understand complex programming logic and code written by others.  
  • Predictive Code Completion: Developers can speed up coding by leveraging code prediction capabilities. Based on context, comments, and previously entered specifications, AI suggests the following line of ABAP code while the developers are still typing. This predictive code completion improves code quality and ensures the generated code is cloud-compliant.  
  • Automated Unit Test Generation: Developers can save time and effort by automatically generating ABAP unit tests for ABAP classes and CDS code.  

In addition to these capabilities, our new software development kit, the ABAP AI SDK (software development kit), powered by ISLM (Intelligent Scenario Lifecycle Management), enables developers to create intelligent scenarios within ABAP code using any GenAI model they choose. This flexibility allows developers to expand solutions, tweak functionalities, and unleash creativity with their ABAP apps and extensions. 

 

Early Adoption and Feedback 

We provided early access to these features to over 6,500 ABAP developers at SAP and select customers and partners. The feedback we’ve received from these developers has been overwhelmingly positive, highlighting the transformative impact that the AI capabilities in ABAP can have on the development lifecycle. Here are some excerpts from the anonymous feedback survey:  

“The capabilities of the language model [knowing ABAP] are very good and beneficial in my daily development. Good Job!” 

“[These new capabilities] are well-structured and allowed me an easy start with Gen AI in ABAP Development Tool. Excellent job!” 

“The code completion is the state-of-the-art feature of Joule's ABAP developer capabilities. It understood what I was coding and suggested the perfect snippets.” 

In addition, we also provided an early briefing on these AI enhancements to a few analyst firms. Here’s what Holger Mueller from Constellation Research, a leading analyst firm, had to say on the AI capabilities in ABAP:  

“It's great to see SAP’s holistic strategy addressing the needs of the millions of ABAP developers. The introduction of proprietary LLMs for ABAP has significant potential, particularly in simplifying the migration from ECC to S/4HANA. The expanded SAP Build offering, now including ABAP, provides customers with a powerful solution to build and extend a wide range of business applications across both SAP and non-SAP systems” - Holger Mueller, Principal Analyst & VP at Constellation Research, Inc. 

 

Our Vision for the Future  

Joule for developers, ABAP AI capabilities, will improve developer workflows and open the door for continued innovation and growth. Especially as customers continue to adopt SAP S/4HANA, our offering will revolutionize the migration journey by meticulously analyzing legacy code, providing clear code explanations, and delivering suggestions for code simplification and proposals for optimized migration. These new AI capabilities for ABAP Cloud aren’t merely an approach for lift-and-shift; instead, these enable intelligent optimization and cloud-centric evolution of business-critical custom codebases.  

Our aspirations don’t stop there. We have a complete roadmap of cutting-edge features to streamline business logic development, automate ABAP doc generation, and fortify data protection and compliance. These upcoming innovations will mark significant steps in a journey towards more innovative, efficient, and secure ABAP development. Stay tuned as we continue to leverage the latest AI innovation to shape the path for successful SAP S/4HANA migration for our customers. 

 

Call to Action 

To learn more, visit SAP Help, learn how to get started, and get more details. Please join the conversation here, in the SAP Community. We welcome feedback and look forward to seeing how these AI features transform your projects.  

Join us on this exciting journey as we redefine the future of ABAP development. With Joule for developers, ABAP AI capabilities, we have a treasure in our hands – a tool that not only streamlines workflows but also opens new possibilities for innovation and growth. 

 

Links

Joule speaks ABAP!

Unleash Your Inner ABAP Rockstar with Joule for developers , ABAP AI capabilities

entry.title: Joule speaks ABAP!
entry.content:
Did you ever learn a new language? Then you know how much training and practical experience is required to know the grammar, learn the vocabulary and become fluent. Joule has mastered this journey now and finally is able to speak ABAP! 😊

 

 

Introduction

“At SAP, the most valuable language is ABAP” – Jensen Huang, Founder and CEO of NVIDIA (Sapphire Key Note 2024).

ABAP is SAP’s proprietary programming language and ABAP technology is the solid foundation for both the classic ERP and SAP’s flagship ERP SAP S/4HANA Cloud. Bundled with our strong ABAP development community, the impact and the value of generative AI support for ABAP is obvious. Consequently, SAP decided to invest heavily in the development of Joule skills as well as training and fine-tuning large language models specifically for ABAP.

Now – more than one year of investment after the initial announcement at SAP TechEd 2023, and the refresher end of 2024, we are super happy and proud to announce the general availability of our Joule copilot for ABAP developers in ABAP Development Tools for Eclipse as well as the ABAP AI SDK powered by the Intelligent Scenario Lifecycle Management!

 

Overview over ABAP AI use cases

Our use cases can be grouped into three categories.

Accelerate: Comprises all Joule skills which make ABAP developers more efficient. Besides the Joule integration in ABAP Development Tools itself, the first scope of skills comprises Predictive Code Completion, Unit Test generation for ABAP classes and CDS Views as well as ABAP and CDS code explanation.

Example for Joule's ABAP CDS Explain skill

 

Empower: We empower ABAP developers to infuse AI capabilities into their solutions and extensions. The ABAP AI SDK powered by the Intelligent Scenario Lifecycle Management (ISLM) is an easy-to-consume ABAP client providing full access to the SAP BTP Generative AI Hub.

Transform: The transformation of customer and partner code from the classic ERP towards SAP S/4HANA and further towards a clean core is one of the major challenges for many of our customers. We will assist with generative AI in this endeavor starting later in 2025.

In this blog post we are now concentrating on the Accelerate and the Empower use case. Further information on the Transform use case will follow later in 2025.

 

Accelerate your ABAP development

Which products are supported?

Joule’s ABAP skills are available in:

  • SAP BTP ABAP Environment
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition as of release 2025.

Supporting lower releases of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition is currently in evaluation.

Get started for free!

License: In order to leverage Joule’s capabilities, you need to purchase an additional license: 8019124 – Joule for developers limited promotion, ABAP. Please check SAP Note 3571857 for details. This license is a limited promotion free of charge until September 15th 2025 and can be purchased until August 15th 2025.

The future commercial model is currently in finalization, updates will follow.

Architecture and Set-Up: The architecture follows the general guiding principles of all Joule capabilities at SAP.

High-level overview over Joule.

 All requests to large language models are routed from ABAP to the AI foundation on SAP BTP. The use cases in the Accelerate category are managed by SAP. Once the subscription explained above is in place, the configuration part in your system is done automatically by SAP. Finally, update your ABAP Development Tools for Eclipse to the latest version to get the Joule integration in the IDE. Developers who shall be allowed to use Joule need the respective authorizations as explained in the documentation.

Learn how to use Joule

Explore Joule’s new ABAP skills by working through our tutorial on github. For details on specific features, check out our documentation.

You wonder what could be the technical foundation in ABAP to integrate Joule in ABAP Development Tools and offer all Joule skills for ABAP developers? It is the ABAP AI SDK powered by ISLM, the main deliverable of the Empower use case. And the good news is, we offer the entire ABAP AI SDK functionality to you as well, to realize your own AI use cases! Continue reading the next section for the details.

 

You are empowered!

With the ABAP AI SDK powered by ISLM we empower you to infuse AI capabilities into your own custom applications and solutions. This way you benefit from the same comfort as SAP for realizing AI use cases in ABAP.

Which products are supported?

The ABAP AI SDK powered by ISLM is available in:

  • SAP BTP ABAP Environment 
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and OnPrem as of release 2025 via standard delivery. Lower releases of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and OnPrem are supported via Transport-based Correction Instructions (TCI), see details in SAP Note 3513374 and the SAP Notes and instructions linked therein.

Get started!

License: The AI capabilities of SAP BTP are offered through the SAP AI Core service. Customers need to have their own instance of SAP AI Core to be used from ABAP via the ABAP AI SDK. There is no separate license or pricing from ABAP perspective, the entire licensing and pricing is according to the SAP AI Core service.

Architecture and Set-Up: The architecture follows the general guiding principles of all Joule capabilities at SAP. All requests to large language models are routed from ABAP to the AI foundation on SAP BTP. For Empower use case, an own instance of SAP AI Core needs to be used and the communication between the ABAP system and this instance needs to be configured. Finally the AI scenarios are managed by the ISLM. Find all details for the ABAP AI SDK with step-by-step instructions in the documentation. For details on the ISLM, please see the list of references given below.

 

Find more information

This is a summary of important sources of further information. More details will follow and will be published and linked here. Please

  • Keep track of ongoing developments and news by following our SAP community.
  • Make use of our FAQ page for generative AI for ABAP to get many frequent questions answered. The FAQ will be updated regularly.
  • Consult the SAP Help documentation for details regarding all features.
  • Find the region mapping between SAP BTP ABAP Environment and SAP AI Core here.
  • Watch the recordings of Devtoberfest and the SAP BTP ABAP Environment Roundtable #19 for demos of the initial scope.
  • Check out the ISLM documentation (or this version for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition) for more information on the Intelligent Scenario Lifecycle Management.
    Visit the ISLM community page for further guidance and watch the corresponding Devtoberfest session.

 

Roadmap

After the initial delivery, more features and skills will follow in upcoming releases. See our roadmap to know what’s already in the planning.

With that, the only thing left to say is: Have fun talking ABAP with Joule! 😊

entry.title: Episode 1: SAP BTP Personas
entry.content:
🎬 Episode 1: SAP BTP Personas 🎬

🔍 Who’s involved in SAP BTP? This episode focuses on the key personas driving SAP BTP success: 👨💻 Developers – Build and enhance applications. 🛠 SAP Basis Administrators – Manage the technical infrastructure. 🔒 Authorization Team – Ensure security & access control. 🧠 Architects – Guide platform innovations and strategic decisions.

⚖️ Why Segregation of Duties Matters Proper role separation is essential for a secure and efficient SAP BTP landscape.

🤝 Collaboration = Success! A multi-disciplinary team is key to ensuring optimal performance, security, and customer satisfaction. Watch to learn how these roles come together to create a robust SAP BTP environment!

📺 Tune in now! 🎥

 

🔥 Takeaways from SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) Governance 🔥

✅ Key Personas in SAP BTP: 👨💻 Developers, architects, basis administrators, and the authorization team are crucial players in SAP BTP.

🔐 Segregation of Duties Matters! Just like in a classical SAP or SAP S/4HANA landscape, proper role separation is essential.

🚫 Developers & Production Data: Developers should not have access to production data to maintain security and functionality.

🌐 Basis Administrators: They provide destinations and connectivity for developers to ensure smooth integration.

🔒 Authorization Team: They manage security and roles, ensuring data protection and access control across SAP BTP.

🧠 Architects & Innovation: Architects keep up with the latest platform innovations and advise customers on which services and products to invest in.

👥 Multi-Disciplinary Teams = Success A well-rounded team with expertise from different domains ensures efficient SAP BTP management.

🔗 Security, Connectivity, and Authorization Each of these must be handled by separate roles for a secure and well-structured platform.

🤝 Collaboration is Key! All team members should work together to ensure the best outcomes for the customer.

#SAPBTP #Governance #SAPSecurity #Cloud #SAP #Teamwork #BestPractices #SAPMentors

entry.title: Re: Data driven engineering change process drives Industry 4.0
entry.content:
informative article, 

one type of change adding new component and deleting current component is fine.
Normally to avoid parts mix-up and traceability we also change assy part number , how does this ECR scenario works ?

entry.title: Re: Custom Business Configuration
entry.content:
Hi @Ram_peddireddi ,

according to the SAP Help Documentation there is no such feature. This means you need to manually extend the CDS Views, Metadata annotation and BDEF.

BR, Attila

entry.title: Re: How to import Test Scripts from the SAP Best Practice content for SAP S/4 HANA Cloud into SAP Cl
entry.content: Is there a solution to copy a test plan with the test cases from one project to another?

entry.title: Generate multiple invoices from a single sales document (Contract)
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

Could you please provide guidance on the following matter?

Is it possible to create multiple invoices from a single sales document? For example, for a contract, I would like to issue a new invoice for the full amount of the contract every year if the customer did not resiliate the contract within three months before the end of the year.

In this case, the contract is for a service (no delivery), and the quantity is 1.

Do I need to use a periodic billing plan for this, or is there another setting that allows for invoicing multiple times?

Best regards!

entry.title: Re: Manage Prices Sales (F4111) - Access the data externally
entry.content:
Hello,

The API is not yet available to retrieve all data in the Manage Prices - Sales app. You either need to create custom OData services or create a feature request.

Best regards,

Dequan Xu

entry.title: LSMW - Legacy System Migration Workbench
entry.content:
LSMW: stands for Legacy System Migration Workbench

The tool is primarily intended for the one-time or periodic transfer of data from external systems (legacy systems) to an SAP system. Thus, it plays a role when switching from third-party systems to SAP products.

The core functions of the SAP Legacy System Migration Workbench are:

  • Importing Data from Non-SAP SystemsConversion of data from source format to SAP format.
  • Import of converted data (including consistency check)

Although the name refers to the migration of legacy data, SAP LSMW provides additional functions for creating and changing master data, transaction data, and Customizing data. From a technical point of view, the following options are available:

  • Batch Input: Create (record) and transfer sessions by running transactions.
  • Direct Input: Direct transfer of data to SAP database tables
  • BAPI: Data transfer via interface
  • IDoc: Data Transfer via Standardized Electronic Messages

The data that can be migrated with the SAP Legacy System Migration Workbench:

LSMW already enables the transfer of numerous master data and transaction data in the standard system. These include G/L accounts, customer master data, supplier master data, material master data and bills of materials. Of course, it is also possible to transfer documents such as orders, purchase orders or posting documents from financial accounting.

Additional data objects can be migrated by recording transactions if the transaction is suitable for the batch input method. This method can also be used for mass deletion of data.

Application of SAP LSMW with Batch Input:
Put simply, the batch input concept simulates the input of data by a human user. To do this, batch input sessions are first created, which contain both the necessary transaction codes (input screens) and the data to be entered. If the data originates from a non-SAP system, it must first be formatted to conform to the SAP input formats.

Select the Batch Input Recording and click on the overview.

The system will take you to the Material Master creation screen and record the data you enter.

Click on the "Default All" tab, and the field names will appear at the specific data that was maintained during the creation of the Material Master.

Click on the back button, and the system will display the recording name in the Batch Input Recording under "Define Object Attributes."

By Maintaining the Record name in the Recording field click on the save.

Click on the object Overview and select the table format

The system Picks the field names automatically by recording while creating Material Master.

Application of SAP LSMW with Direct Input:

In contrast to batch input, no sessions are generated with the direct input procedure. The data is updated directly. This means that no screens are run through. Instead, the data is written directly to the target database tables. If errors occur, a restart function can be used. Direct inputs can only be processed in the background to ensure that this is effective.

Direct input is particularly suitable for very large amounts of data, since it offers considerably better performance than batch input. SAP function modules are also available here for consistency checks.

We have to Manually enter the field names in XL Sheet or Notepad.

The Benefits of SAP LSMW are:

  1. The main advantage lies in the considerable reduction of the time and cost involved in data migrations.
  2. Additional simplifications are provided by the reusability of data mapping and conversion rules.
  3. Another positive aspect is that migration can be carried out without programming.
  4. Special knowledge (e.g., ABAP) is only required if very specific steps are to be mapped. Users are guided intuitively through the migration process by a clear sequence of steps.
  5. Another advantage is that LSMW ensures a high level of data quality and consistency through import checks. If the procedure is correct, serious damage to the existing SAP database caused by incorrect imports can be almost ruled out.

Process Flow:

Enter the Transaction Code – LSMW in the Command Field

The Initial screen may appear like this the first step you need to do is Create Project, Sub Project and Object as shown in the screen below:

Maintain the project name and click on Add entry button as shown.

Project can have multiple Subprojects and in turn Subprojects can have multiple Objects as explained below:

By Maintaining the project, subproject and object click on the Tickmark.

Click on the Execute.

In these some fields are optional and some fields will be automatically assigned.

Select the line Define Object Attributes and Click on the Execute.

It will be always in display mode we have to change to change mode manually by clicking on Display <–> Change tab in every step.

Here I’m creating the Materials by using Batch Input Recording.

Select the Batch input Recording and click on the overview. Click on Create Recording.

Here we can maintain the input recording name and description click the enter or click on the Check box which is shown below with the Tick mark.

The system asks to enter the Transaction Code. Enter MM01.

The system will take you to the Material Master creation screen and record the data you enter.

After saving the material using batch input recording, the initial screen in LSMW will appear as follows: Click on "Default All," and the system will display the field names that were maintained during the creation of the Material Master.

Click on the back button.

Click on again the back button.

By Maintaining the Record in the field click on the save button.

Some fields will disappear automatically.

Select Define Source structures and click on Execute.

Click on "New Entry" to maintain the source structure and description. Then, click the tick mark and then click on save.

Next, click on "Object Overview" and select the table format.

 

Here, the system displays the fields that can be maintained during the creation of the Material Master.

Select the line Define Source fields and click on Execute.

Place the cursor on source structure and click on the Table Maintenance.

Maintain the fields in the source field that correspond to the data entered during the creation of the Material Master.

The fields are assigned to the Source structure by checking the data click on save.

Select the line Define Structure Relations and click on Execute.

The system automatically assigns the source structure to Recording which is created in the batch input Recording.

Select the line Define Field Mapping and Conversion Rules and click on Execute

Place the cursor on the batch recording name, go to "Extras" in the header, and click on "Auto Field Mapping."

Click on "Auto Field Mapping," and the screen will open. Do not change any data; simply click on "Enter."

It will display the fields that were maintained during the creation of the Material Master using the Batch Input recording. Here, simply check the field names and click on the "Accept Proposal" tab.

By checking the data click on the save button.

Select the line Specify Files and click on Execute.

Place the cursor on legacy data and click on Add Entry

Upload the file which is maintained in the Notepad and maintain the name which is mandatory.

  • Select the Tabulator if we maintain data in the Notepad.
  • If We maintained data in XL sheet, select comma tab.

Select the checkbox "Field Names at Start of File" if the field names are maintained in the document that you uploaded in this step.

After specifying the file click on the save button.

Select the line Assign files and click on the Execute.

The system automatically assigns the file to the source Structure. Go to the change mode and click on save.

Select Read Data and Click on Execute button.

Click on the Execute, the system displays a pop up to allow or deny for further steps.

Click on the allow the system picks the data from file that we assigned.

In the file we maintain the data for 3 Materials.

Select Display Read data and click on Execute button.

Click on the Enter or the Tick mark which is shown below.

Select the line, convert data and click on the Execute button.

Click on the Execute.

Select the line Display Converted Data and click on Execute.

Click the Enter button or Tick mark tab which is shown below.

This is the last step if you want to make any changes to the data. Double click on the line item or select the line item and click on the field contents to see the full details.

Select the line Create Batch Input Section and click on the Execute button.

Select the Keep batch input folder check box and click on Execute.

Under 1 batch 3 transactions are created.

Select the line Run Batch Input Session and click on the Execute button

Select the line item and click on Process the system will open the screen.

Process/Foreground:

If we select this indicator, the process will be done in the front end.

Display Errors only:

If we select this indicator, the system directly shows if the data contains any errors.

Background:

If we select this indicator, the whole process will be done in the background.

  • Here I am selecting the Process/Foreground indicator I.e., the process will be the front end.
  • By selecting the required check box (Extended log and Expert mode) click on the process.

Keep Pressing on Enter

After Completing of creation materials, the system shows the pop-up message that Processing of batch input session completed.

Click on the session overview field which is shown below.

The Materials are created successfully.

Conclusion: 

I think this blog will helpful for someone and in summary, LSMW (Legacy System Migration Workbench) is a crucial tool in SAP for migrating data from legacy systems to SAP. It allows users to efficiently transfer data through a structured process of recording, mapping, and uploading. By automating the data import process, LSMW ensures data accuracy and integrity during migration. Its ease of use and flexibility make it an indispensable tool for successful data migration within SAP systems.

entry.title: Re: Devtoberfest Winners Revealed
entry.content: Well done to everyone who took part and big congratulations to all the worthy winners 🎉

entry.title: Re: Navigation button on object page in fiori elements list report standalone deployment
entry.content: I know this is super old but we have the same question. Were you able to solve this?

entry.title: Re: Unleash Your Inner ABAP Rockstar with Joule for developers , ABAP AI capabilities
entry.content: Any plans to bring this to private Cloud, on premise or older systems like ECC?

entry.title: Re: Sales areas of Bill-To Party and the service transact...
entry.content:
Hello,

The error message "Sales areas of Bill-To Party and the service transaction don’t match" indicates that there is a mismatch between the sales area data of the bill-to party (your business partner) and the service transaction you are trying to create.

In SAP S/4HANA, sales areas are combinations of sales organization, distribution channel, and division. Each business partner (such as a customer) can be assigned to specific sales areas, and these assignments need to match the sales area data in your service order or transaction.

Here are some potential reasons for this issue:

1. **Sales Area Mismatch**: The business partner you are using as the bill-to party may not have the same sales area configuration as the one defined in the service transaction. Ensure that the sales organization, distribution channel, and division of the bill-to party match those specified in the service order.

2. **Incomplete Sales Area Assignment**: Check if the business partner has been correctly assigned to all necessary sales areas. You can verify this by reviewing the sales area data on the business partner master record.

3. **Incorrect Role Assignment**: Ensure that the business partner roles, such as FLCU00, FLCU01, and CRM00, have the appropriate sales area details. The roles might have different sales area data or configurations that need to align with your service transaction.

To resolve this issue, you should:

- Review the sales area data in the business partner master record to ensure it's complete and matches the service transaction requirements.
- Verify and, if necessary, update the sales area data in either the service order or the business partner setup so that they align.

Best regards,

Dequan Xu

entry.title: PaymentInformation upsert in SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central through CPI
entry.content:
Hi All,

I have a scenario where I have to update the bank details coming in a csv file to PaymentInformation portlet in Successfactors Employee Central to an entity PaymentInformationDetailV3.

We are receiving all the details except external code from source system. The external code is a key field in the PaymentInformationDetailV3, So I am unable to update the information with the below error.

PaymentInformationDetailV3 Upsert Failed for empId: 00002. Error msg: bad valueString [] as part of keyString [PaymentInformationV3_effectiveStartDate=datetime'2025-01-03T00%3A00%3A00',PaymentInformationV3_worker='00002',externalCode=]"

I have checked the below KBA's as well.

https://me.sap.com/notes/3000179
https://me.sap.com/notes/0002990468

The external code is an autogenerated field in Employee central, When the SF EC consultant update the information manually through import templates the external code is auto generated.

Please let me know how this field external code is mapped in sap cpi .

Regards,

Ramya

entry.title: LSMW - Legacy System Migration Workbench
entry.content:
LSMW: stands for Legacy System Migration Workbench

The tool is primarily intended for the one-time or periodic transfer of data from external systems (legacy systems) to an SAP system. Thus, it plays a role when switching from third-party systems to SAP products.

The core functions of the SAP Legacy System Migration Workbench are:

  • Importing Data from Non-SAP Systems Conversion of data from source format to SAP format.
  • Import of converted data (including consistency check)

Although the name refers to the migration of legacy data, SAP LSMW provides additional functions for creating and changing master data, transaction data, and Customizing data. From a technical point of view, the following options are available:

  • Batch Input: Create (record) and transfer sessions by running transactions.
  • Direct Input: Direct transfer of data to SAP database tables
  • BAPI: Data transfer via interface
  • IDoc: Data Transfer via Standardized Electronic Messages

The data that can be migrated with the SAP Legacy System Migration Workbench:

LSMW already enables the transfer of numerous master data and transaction data in the standard system. These include G/L accounts, customer master data, supplier master data, material master data and bills of materials. Of course, it is also possible to transfer documents such as orders, purchase orders or posting documents from financial accounting.

Additional data objects can be migrated by recording transactions if the transaction is suitable for the batch input method. This method can also be used for mass deletion of data.

Application of SAP LSMW with Batch Input:
Put simply, the batch input concept simulates the input of data by a human user. To do this, batch input sessions are first created, which contain both the necessary transaction codes (input screens) and the data to be entered. If the data originates from a non-SAP system, it must first be formatted to conform to the SAP input formats.

Select the Batch Input Recording and click on the overview.

The system will take you to the Material Master creation screen and record the data you enter.

Click on the "Default All" tab, and the field names will appear at the specific data that was maintained during the creation of the Material Master.

Click on the back button, and the system will display the recording name in the Batch Input Recording under "Define Object Attributes."

By Maintaining the Record name in the Recording field click on the save.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Click on the object Overview and select the table format

The system Picks the field names automatically by recording while creating Material Master.

 

Application of SAP LSMW with Direct Input:

In contrast to batch input, no sessions are generated with the direct input procedure. The data is updated directly. This means that no screens are run through. Instead, the data is written directly to the target database tables. If errors occur, a restart function can be used. Direct inputs can only be processed in the background to ensure that this is effective.

Direct input is particularly suitable for very large amounts of data, since it offers considerably better performance than batch input. SAP function modules are also available here for consistency checks.

We have to Manually enter the field names in XL Sheet or Notepad.

The Benefits of SAP LSMW are:

  1. The main advantage lies in the considerable reduction of the time and cost involved in data migrations.
  2. Additional simplifications are provided by the reusability of data mapping and conversion rules.
  3. Another positive aspect is that migration can be carried out without programming.
  4. Special knowledge (e.g., ABAP) is only required if very specific steps are to be mapped. Users are guided intuitively through the migration process by a clear sequence of steps.
  5. Another advantage is that LSMW ensures a high level of data quality and consistency through import checks. If the procedure is correct, serious damage to the existing SAP database caused by incorrect imports can be almost ruled out.

 

Process Flow:

Enter the Transaction Code – LSMW in the Command Field

The Initial screen may appear like this the first step you need to do is Create Project, Sub Project and Object as shown in the screen below:

Maintain the project name and click on Add entry button as shown.

Project can have multiple Subprojects and in turn Subprojects can have multiple Objects as explained below:

By Maintaining the project, subproject and object click on the Tickmark.

Click on the Execute.

In these some fields are optional and some fields will be automatically assigned.

Select the line Define Object Attributes and Click on the Execute.

It will be always in display mode we have to change to change mode manually by clicking on Display <–> Change tab in every step.

Here I’m creating the Materials by using Batch Input Recording.

Select the Batch input Recording and click on the overview. Click on Create Recording.

Here we can maintain the input recording name and description click the enter or click on the Check box which is shown below with the Tick mark.

The system asks to enter the Transaction Code. Enter MM01.

The system will take you to the Material Master creation screen and record the data you enter.

After saving the material using batch input recording, the initial screen in LSMW will appear as follows: Click on "Default All," and the system will display the field names that were maintained during the creation of the Material Master.

Click on the back button.

Click on again the back button.

By Maintaining the Record in the field click on the save button.

Some fields will disappear automatically.

Select Define Source structures and click on Execute.

Click on "New Entry" to maintain the source structure and description. Then, click the tick mark and then click on save.

Next, click on "Object Overview" and select the table format.

Here, the system displays the fields that can be maintained during the creation of the Material Master.

Select the line Define Source fields and click on Execute.

Place the cursor on source structure and click on the Table Maintenance.

Maintain the fields in the source field that correspond to the data entered during the creation of the Material Master.

The fields are assigned to the Source structure by checking the data click on save.

Select the line Define Structure Relations and click on Execute.

The system automatically assigns the source structure to Recording which is created in the batch input Recording.

Select the line Define Field Mapping and Conversion Rules and click on Execute

Place the cursor on the batch recording name, go to "Extras" in the header, and click on "Auto Field Mapping."

Click on "Auto Field Mapping," and the screen will open. Do not change any data; simply click on "Enter."

It will display the fields that were maintained during the creation of the Material Master using the Batch Input recording. Here, simply check the field names and click on the "Accept Proposal" tab.

By checking the data click on the save button.

Select the line Specify Files and click on Execute.

Place the cursor on legacy data and click on Add Entry

Upload the file which is maintained in the Notepad and maintain the name which is mandatory.

  • Select the Tabulator if we maintain data in the Notepad.
  • If We maintained data in XL sheet, select comma tab.

Select the checkbox "Field Names at Start of File" if the field names are maintained in the document that you uploaded in this step.

After specifying the file click on the save button.

Select the line Assign files and click on the Execute.

The system automatically assigns the file to the source Structure. Go to the change mode and click on save.

Select Read Data and Click on Execute button.

Click on the Execute, the system displays a pop up to allow or deny for further steps.

Click on the allow the system picks the data from file that we assigned.

In the file we maintain the data for 3 Materials.

Select Display Read data and click on Execute button.

Click on the Enter or the Tick mark which is shown below.

Select the line, convert data and click on the Execute button.

Click on the Execute.

Select the line Display Converted Data and click on Execute.

Click the Enter button or Tick mark tab which is shown below.

This is the last step if you want to make any changes to the data. Double click on the line item or select the line item and click on the field contents to see the full details.

Select the line Create Batch Input Section and click on the Execute button.

Select the Keep batch input folder check box and click on Execute.

Under 1 batch 3 transactions are created.

Select the line Run Batch Input Session and click on the Execute button

Select the line item and click on Process the system will open the screen.

Process/Foreground:

If we select this indicator, the process will be done in the front end.

Display Errors only:

If we select this indicator, the system directly shows if the data contains any errors.

Background:

If we select this indicator, the whole process will be done in the background.

  • Here I am selecting the Process/Foreground indicator I.e., the process will be the front end.
  • By selecting the required check box (Extended log and Expert mode) click on the process.

Keep Pressing on Enter

After Completing of creation materials, the system shows the pop-up message that Processing of batch input session completed.

Click on the session overview field which is shown below.

The Materials are created successfully.

Conclusion: 

I think this blog will helpful for someone and in summary, LSMW (Legacy System Migration Workbench) is a crucial tool in SAP for migrating data from legacy systems to SAP. It allows users to efficiently transfer data through a structured process of recording, mapping, and uploading. By automating the data import process, LSMW ensures data accuracy and integrity during migration. Its ease of use and flexibility make it an indispensable tool for successful data migration within SAP systems.

entry.title: Re: Creation of a generic address report at embedded People Analytics
entry.content:
Hi,

This is very useful, thank you!

A little detail about "enabling" the state field in Generic address object - it is enough to add the state field in the HRIS field part of the Generic address object and configure it as a picklist.

The state field does not necessarily need to have "Yes" in its "Enabled" property to make this work.

Thanks again for this post, it is really a great help for us!

Regards,

entry.title: Re: YCOA Chart of Account
entry.content:
Hi Rachel,

Thanks for your reply! Then we would have a look at the implementation status to make sure only the accounts we want to use are in P-status. 
Great possibility that we most certain will be using with our customer. 

Kind Regards, Sanne

entry.title: SAP Analytics Cloud - Threshold Visualization Issue
entry.content:
Hi Friends,

currently I'am observing following issue with visualizing thresholds. 

I set up the threshold like this:

1. 

2. 

3. 

Now. When I save the story and open it in View mode the throsholds aren't shown. 

View Mode:

I believe the issue might be related to the threshold setting, as the Measure under Step 2 is populated by an element from the "FIBUPe Time Slices" dimension. However, this element is not actually a measure.

What I don't understand is—why does it work in edit mode?

Can anyone explain?

Kind regards,

Ismail

entry.title: Re: SFW5 how to inactivate
entry.content: It seems that you can't. Only reversible business functions can be deactivated. It's exactly like switches. It's why you must be careful, for instance have a sandbox. For more information, see 2515814 - Deactivate Business Functions in SFW5 - SAP for Me.

entry.title: Re: Part 2: Connect to on-premise APIs from SAP Cloud Platform API Management Cloud Foundry Environm
entry.content:
Hi,
While creating the API Provider URL is showing as HTTP. Even though we have done the CC configuration with https port. Below are the screen shot for same

entry.title: Re: cannot change ABAP program
entry.content:
The line """"""""""""""""""""""""" means it's part of the Enhancement Framework. Precisely, it's a block defined at an Implicit Enhancement Option, which is here at the Beginning of Method.

  1. Use this whirl button to switch to the Enhancement Editor:
  2. Right-click on the line ENHANCEMENT to see all edit options (especially extension/enhancement options > change)

Note that here it's standard that you want to change.

entry.title: Re: FP_JOB_OPEN
entry.content: It happens because when the interface type is set to 'Smartform Compatible Interface'. Then in the generated function module, we get an additional call to FPCOMP_JOB_OPEN. Change the interface type to 'ABAP Dictionary based interface'. That will solve the problem

entry.title: Re: Create/Restore Postgres DB Backup
entry.content: Hi, can you please open a support ticket using the component BC-CP-BSB-POSTGRES (please see how to https://help.sap.com/docs/PostgreSQL/b3fe3621fa4a4ed28d7bbe3d6d88f036/monitoring-and-troubleshooting). Add information regarding your global account Id, instance Id, region and the command line that you are executing to do the restore. I'll request the dev team to look into it. Regards, Antonio

entry.title: Re: Process chain run into error with Error in remote cal...
entry.content:
Hi Fanny

 

  1. What kind of process is exactly failing with that error?
  2. Is the RFC destination PS1CLNT800 working correctly? (connection and authorization test from SM59)
  3. What exactly is the error in PS1?
    • Dump?
    • Job cancellation?
    • Database error?
    • Some other error message?

I also would suggest to check with your basis as well.

br

Roland

entry.title: Re: Issue while implementing SAP Note 3501652
entry.content: Note "cannot be implemented" means that it's already implemented.

entry.title: Re: SAP Standard Kafka Integration Options
entry.content:
Hello AngeloRuggiero,

Have you already tried the Kafka adapter of the Integration Suite? But it cannot be exposed via the Cloud Connector. It is currently only available between cloud applications.

Additionally, there are  also Kafka add-ons from the companys Avato or Init.

Kind regards,

Stefan

entry.title: Re: LINK TO WEBGUI IN TUTORIAL IS NOT WORKING ANYMORE?
entry.content: Please be explicit, which "first link" doesn't work? The first link I can find works well for me.

entry.title: Re: I need production order cost with all Production issu...
entry.content: Thanks dear @Manish I want only document date filtration

entry.title: Advance G/L Account determination issue facing into SAP B1
entry.content:
Dear All,

I hope this email finds you well.

I am facing an issue while performing the Account Determination to Advanced G/L Account Determination process in the live database. Specifically, only a few Item Groups and corresponding G/L Accounts are appearing in the Advanced G/L Account Determination screen, instead of all the expected item groups and G/L accounts.

We had selected the option "Migrate Account Assignments and Stock G/L Account Determination Settings" during migration, but it seems that some mappings are missing.

SAP Business One SQL version, SAP B1

Regards,

Nikunj

entry.title: Best Practice for Productive Indicator During Asset Migration (ECC 6.0 → S/4HANA)
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

We are currently migrating from SAP ECC 6.0 to S/4HANA and are in the post-SUM activities, specifically the customizing migration for financial accounting and data migration.

During the asset migration, we encountered an issue where the system did not allow the migration due to the productive indicator being set. As a workaround, we temporarily changed the productive indicator to "Test" to proceed with the migration.

Now, we are wondering:
What is the best practice during the migration?

  • Should the productive indicator be set to "Test" for the entire migration process?
  • Are there alternative recommended approaches to handle this restriction?

We would appreciate any insights and recommendations based on your experience!

entry.title: Re: Manage Prices Sales (F4111) - Access the data externally
entry.content:
Thanks for the quick reply! When you say 'not yet available', does that mean it is planned? And if so, what release would be feasible?

Creating a custom OData service would be great, but the CDS views that are required to access that data are not released for that use. We are not allowed to access the services SAP makes use of internally, same goes for the underlying CDS views. Otherwise, that custom OData would be the obvious option.

A completely different approach would be to create an Application Job, which extracts the data using the local interface (if allowed), store that in a custom table... which we can then release to create an OData service. Not real-time, not nice, but potentially the option we have.

entry.title: Moving to a New Cost Center – How to Update Master Data for Assets, WBS, etc.?
entry.content:
We are in the process of transitioning from an existing (old) cost center to a new cost center structure within our SAP environment. Our objective is to update all relevant master data objects impacted by this change to ensure consistency and accuracy across our system.

Our Scenario:

  • Current Situation:
    Our old cost center is integrated with various master data objects (including cost center hierarchies, Profitability Analysis (CO-PA), internal orders, budgeting reports, and asset accounting). The need to replace it with a new cost center has arisen due to organizational restructuring.

  • Goal:
    Seamlessly replace the old cost center with the new one across all master data, ensuring that all dependencies, historical data, and reporting functionalities remain intact

Our Key Questions:

  1. Migration Process:

    • What is the recommended process for updating cost center master data from an old to a new cost center?
    • Are there specific SAP tools, transactions, or methodologies (e.g., LSMW, batch input, BAPIs) that can automate or streamline this process?

  2. Impact on Linked Objects:

    • How should we approach updating cost center references in related areas such as CO-PA segments, internal orders, asset accounting, and other integrated modules?
    • What potential pitfalls should we be aware of when updating these interconnected master data objects?
  3. Handling Historical Data:
  • What considerations should we take into account regarding historical data that may still reference the old cost center?
  • Is there a need to maintain a linkage or mapping for audit purposes, or should historical data be updated as well?

Any insights, experiences, or detailed recommendations you could share would be invaluable as we plan this transition. 

Thank you in advance for your assistance!

entry.title: Re: cannot change ABAP program
entry.content:
@Sandra_Rossi I just would like to comment all lines , put "*" on the front. how to pls?

entry.title: Re: Introduction to Monte Carlo Simulation
entry.content:
HI @MickeyM !

Compass is available for users with the SAP Analytics Cloud planning license.
Also, the role permission needs to be set up to allow compass simulation. You will need to configure it according to the desired access:

Cheers,
Chen Nee

entry.title: Re: HOW TO DISABLE OR GREYOUT SINGLE CELL CHECKBOX WHEN C...

found the solution 
use field symbols and CL_GUI_ALV_GRID=>MC_STYLE_DISABLED on condition of user

Cheers,
Chen Nee

entry.title: create source list ME01 using IDOC/other methods in abap report
entry.content:
i want to create update and delete source list in me01.

i have used DMC_MIG_SOURCE_LIST but its not fulfilling my purpose. 
Is there any other way to create it like idoc or bapi ?

entry.title: How to enable Cloud Identity Services for Kyma runtime
entry.content:
Hi experts,

I try to update an existing Kyma runtime using the SAP Cloud Identity Services as Custom Identity Provider.
I followed the blog Configure Custom SAP IAS tenant with SAP BTP Kyma runtime environment

So, I created a new namespace in my Kyma environment. Here I created a service instance using offering name "identity" and plan name "application" setting following parameters:

assertion-attributes:
  email: mail
  first_name: firstName
  groups: companyGroups
  last_name: lastName
  locale: language
  login_name: loginName
  mail: mail
  scope: companyGroups
  user_uuid: userUuid
default-attributes: {}
display-name: Kyma runtime
home-url: https://api.#######.kyma.ondemand.com
name: ias-kyma-#######
oauth2-configuration:
  grant-types:
    - authorization_code
    - authorization_code_pkce_s256
  public-client: true
  redirect-uris:
    - https://dashboard.kyma.cloud.sap
    - http://localhost:8000
  token-policy:
    access-token-format: default
    refresh-parallel: 3
    token-validity: 3600
subject-name-identifier:
  attribute: mail
  fallback-attribute: none
user-access: internal

Based on this service instance I created a service binding called "skr-ias-binding" with secret name "skr-ias-binding-secret" and following parameters:

{
  "credential-type": "NONE"
}

Once the service binding was provisioned I copied the ClientId and Url from the secret and updated the Kyma runtime using following JSON:

{
    "oidc": {
        "issuerURL": "https://ouriastenant.accounts.ondemand.com",
        "clientID": "########-####-####-####-############",
        "usernameClaim": "sub",
        "groupsClaim": "groups",
        "signingAlgs": [
			"RS256"
		],
        "usernamePrefix": "-"
    },
    "administrators": [
        "user1@mycompany.com",
        "user2@mycompany.com"
    ]
}

The Kyma runtime was updated successfully. And when I launch the dashboard I'm also asked to log in with my IAS user.

However, after login I see following error:

When I check the network trace within the developer tools of the browser I see lot of HTTP 401 errors telling me I'm not authorized. But when I decode the Bearer token attached to the requests I can see all my users details. The "sub" attribute of the Bearer token contains my email which is also configured to be an administrator of the Kyma environment.

Within the IAS admin console I can see the new application. From the configuration it looks good as also described in SAP Help:

Redirect URIs are set to "https://dashboard.kyma.cloud.sap" and "http://localhost:8000" 
The grant types "Authorization code" and "Enforce PKCE" are checked (but no other grant type)
The Public Client is enabled

 

Did I miss a step?

regards

René

entry.title: Variable Pay Budget Calculation
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I am trying to understand the budget calculation for Variable Pay. More specific, we want the the budget to be allocated in a compensation manager, so that why we use the custom field way.  We noticed that at the budget field the total has an unexpected amount, that it the same with the Business calc amount field. Should this be linked? And why is linked? 

Also, in the final pay out field, when we enter an amount into the override field, we noticed that the pay out field take the same amount with Business Calc Amount. The Forecast Budget and Modeling Budget are necessary? We can just use the Result Budget?

I would appreciate your advice.

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Learning Strategies for Complex Groovy Scripts and Graphical Mapping
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I have a question regarding building knowledge about Groovy Script or Graphical Mapping. Both can become very complex during message transformations due to context changes or complex customer requirements. My question is, is there a good way to learn this? Are there any reference books, an SAP course focusing on this, or blueprints for  recurring message transformation available? Or have you gained your experience exclusively through practice in your projects?

Kind regards,

Stefan

entry.title: Re: General Release of SAP Analytics Cloud compass
entry.content:
HI @Sahoo_CG !
Thanks for reaching out! I am not so sure if I understood exactly how a list of deprecated features would help prepare for compass. This new Monte Carlo simulation is not a replacement for any deprecated feature. 
To keep updated on what's new, you can try our What's new viewer for the latest product updates for recent releases.

Cheers!
Chen Nee

entry.title: Re: Archive the previous years' bank statements up to 30-...
entry.content:
Hi,

 

in the future please add your release to the question.

You find all information actually in the help portal: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/918bca53037f408f91a2295d04ac16bc/752ce9df573540f8a631ca5c85f6febf.html

 

Best,

Edrilan Berisha

entry.title: Generative AI capabilities in ABAP— Your Input Needed
entry.content:
AI-powered coding assistance is here for ABAP, and we want your feedback.

New generative AI capabilities are designed to help you write, optimize, and test ABAP code more efficiently. From generating code suggestions to automating unit tests, AI is becoming a powerful tool in the developer workflow. 

How to access the ABAP AI capabilities

You can subscribe to ABAP AI capabilities for no charge until September 15, 2025. This offer is available to developers licensed for SAP BTP ABAP Environment and SAP S/4HANA Cloud, pubic edition (developer user).

If you need help enabling the ABAP AI capabilities, contact your SAP account executive.

We’d love to hear from you 

  • Have you tried AI-driven assistance in your ABAP projects? 
  • What’s working well, and what could be improved? 
  • What AI-powered features would make your development process even better? 

 👉🏼 Learn more

  • The Power of Joule for Developers, ABAP AI Capabilities
  • Joule speaks ABAP!

Your insights will help shape future enhancements— let us know your thoughts in the comments below.

entry.title: Re: How to import Test Scripts from the SAP Best Practice content for SAP S/4 HANA Cloud into SAP Cl
entry.content:
Hi Bruno,

this is not possible yet. Feel free to create an improvement request in our Customer Influence portal:

https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/campaign/3501

Best Regards Reiner

entry.title: Re: HANA Query to track how many sales documents each use...
entry.content: That is perfect thank you, I now need to add if possible a date range, I tried adding T2."DocDate" in the WHERE clause but it doesn't like it

entry.title: Re: create source list ME01 using IDOC/other methods in a...
entry.content:
Did you already try

  • IDOC SRCLST / SRCLST01 

At least IDOC should provide the Insert function

Else use following FM (unreleased) to replicate IDOC_INPUT_SCRLST behavior

  • ME_INITIALIZE_SOURCE_LIST
  • ME_DIRECT_INPUT_SOURCE_LIST
  • ME_POST_SOURCE_LIST_NEW
  • ME_WRITE_DISP_RECORD_SOS
  • COMMIT WORK AND WAIT

For BAPI, I only know a read one BAPI_SOURCEDETERMIN_GETSOS

entry.title: SAP Business One field mapping with FSM Cloud
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We are trying to find out how can we map the user defined field/ Standard fields with SAP Business One from SAP FSM. There is one standard field called as "Serial No."  for service call in SAP Business One which we are not getting in SAP FSM.

We would like to know where and how to do the configurations, is it in  FSM Cloud connector or SAP Business One side.

Could you please let us know how to achieve it to resolve the issue.

 

Thanks,

Supriya Gupta

entry.title: Re: BP CVI - Error : The number ranges for account group & grouping are different
entry.content: i too have the same issue when i uncheck same number message is "You are only permitted to use grouping with identical numbers". both account group and bp grouping have same number range any idea how to resolve ?

entry.title: Re: Advance G/L Account determination issue facing into S...
entry.content:
Hi @nikunjmehta2290 ,

Do you have a backup from the state before migration? You could restore it on a staging environment and check if you have any conflicts, e.g., Inconsistencies in account assignments (e.g., conflicting settings at item, warehouse, or group levels), some accounts may not migrate properly. 

You could also use the "Preview Advanced G/L Account Determination Rules" option to simulate the migration and identify accounts that might be skipped.

Additionally, you could reverse the process (using a restored backup in staging), activate it again and monitor results.

No obvious answer here, but I hope it helps you somehow. Good luck!

BR,
Felipe

entry.title: Re: SAP Global Trade Management (GTM) - Trading Contract (T/C)
entry.content:
Hi Nobuhiro,

I would like to check how do we configure/troubleshoot document flow display issue. I have created a trading contract (purch side) and performed all logistics steps but the document flow only showed upto Material Document from PO. 

Incoming Invoice and Accounting Document have been posted against the PO.

Thanks.

entry.title: Change Display of Quantity Fields in specific language SAP Build Workzone
entry.content:
Hi,

I want to change the display of quantity fields in SAP Build Workzone for a specific language. For example, when you want to display a value of PC they will be displayed with decimal places like "1,000 PC" for one PC is available in Stock. In English language it will displayed "1 PC" without any decimal places. We also want the same display in every language.

Best regards,

Nico

entry.title: Re: How to get Variant Management tile in Build Work Zone?
entry.content: Thank you for the comment. I am aware that I can open it within SAP Build apps. But to make it be a tile created in Build Work Zone is still unfigured..

entry.title: How to setup External URL for SAP Business One between NetBios Vs FQDN
entry.content:
Dear All,

If we deploy SAP B1 on Private Cloud, Which option should we do between 

Option1:  Use NetBios and enter IP > HostName (Public IP) in File Host .
Remark: we can connect from internal network, if we connect from external network will redirect to Public IP in File Host

Option2: Use FQDN without enter IP > HostName in File Host .

Remark: we can connect from external network directly.

 

Best Regards,

Peeraput N.

 

A one of all Point of Concern is security.

Please advise me. Thank you.


Best Regards,

Peeraput N.

entry.title: The substitution of the app : Post Incoming Payments for Customer
entry.content:
After the latest upgrade on S/4HANA Public Cloud, what is the replacement for the Post Incoming Payments for Customer app?

Our clients are struggling with the Payment on Residual Item process.

 

Thanks in advance!

entry.content: @Sandra_Rossi I got the message "Put the cursor within the enhancement implementation"

entry.title: Re: Not Able to update Sales Pricing Data Dynamically for Different Pricing Conditions
entry.content:
Hi ,

Could you provide the custom coding and relevant configuration details? We could help to check.

entry.title: Re: Second DEV Environment in SAP BTP
entry.content:
After Discussion with SAP our solution for this problem is to introduce a new subaccount for the second new release of our Applications.

Another solution could be to use different branches in Software Components App. But we wanted to develop in parallel on both developments which is not possible because you can only have one branch active at one time.

entry.title: Re: create source list ME01 using IDOC/other methods in a...
entry.content: (else go the old way with batch input)

entry.title: Average for 6 months of current year is calculated and its copied as forecast of future year sacp
entry.content: we have to calculate average of Amount for 7 months of current year and copy to forecast of future year for al periods.

entry.title: Re: SAC Planning
entry.content: Hi Nikhil,

entry.title: Re: Display a new key figure as a weighted sum based on a...
entry.content:
@VirginiaToledo 

I think you will have to adjust the Exception Aggregation of this Calculated Measure. You could define it as Sum or any other based on what is right for you and in the Exception aggregation dimension you define the dimension on which you want the exception to take place.

 

Nikhil

entry.title: Need suggestion in workflow configuration in SAP Convergent Mediation
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

I have a situation where I'm retrieving data from a custom S4 table in CM. After processing the data, we update its status in the same table, whether the outcome is successful or a failure. Once the update is complete, we send the data to CI via CC to generate BITs, and there's a pulse agent involved in this workflow. Currently, we trigger the workflow whenever there's data in the S4 table, but I want it to run every second to fetch data in real-time instead of running it manually. Additionally, I'm encountering errors in the system logs after reading data from the S4 table, and I want to prevent these errors in the production environment when I implement the real-time workflow.

Agent name: SAP_CC_Online_2
Message: UNKNOWN UDR TYPE.

 

#BRIM SAP Convergent Mediation by DigitalRoute SAP Billing and Revenue Innovation Management

entry.title: Re: cannot change ABAP program
entry.content: Sorry, you will see the line "ENHANCEMENT", it's on that line that you must right-click.

entry.title: Re: hana backup schedule
entry.content:
Hello

You can see view M_SCHEDULER_JOBS

select * from M_SCHEDULER_JOBS where STATUS='SCHEDULED';

The job parameters:
select * from SCHEDULER_JOB_PARAMETERS;

and the called procedures

select * from SCHEDULER_JOBS;

Regards

Marco

entry.content: Hi Dipender, Were you able to resolve this?

entry.content: @Sandra_Rossi I have try right click on line with """""" (quote) and right click to "change implementation" but still same message that put the cursor within enhancement

entry.title: Re: SAC Planning
entry.content: Hi Nikhil, we have gl account scoa in rows and companycode,functional area,profitcenter,version,time ,amount in columns.when ever user enters certain value in plan version of particular period,value should disaggreate as per percentage of actuals of the particular period.ex:if he enters on april plan version,it should split as per actuals of previous year.

entry.title: Re: How to pass multiple values to function import in oda...
entry.content:
This is exactly what I was looking for. However, listing down the key points here for reference for anyone with a similar requirement.
1. Function can't receive deep parameter since function parameters comes in OData URL

2. Action can receive deep parameter since multiple action parameters can be passed in request body.

3. Deep parameter only supported in OData V4.

entry.title: Handling ONB2.0 rehires in SailPoint
entry.content:
Dear All,

My customer has SailPoint as the Identity Provisioning agent for the Company Idp (Azure AD). After activating ONB2.0, customer had to update a lot of logics and build was somewhat a rebuilt in SailPoint. Rehire scenario was one of the pain points. If the rehire date was in the past and the ONB activity was not completed, Employee Status from EC picks up as active. This means Old employee record gets activated before the actual hire in EC. According to the KBA https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3414839 -this is standard behavior. 

One more thing: SailPoint uses CompoundEmployee API and Odata API. They are unable to put a filter using both APIs.

Has anyone experienced this issue and how did you overcome it?

SAP SuccessFactors Platform , SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding , SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central , API

entry.content: vijay, Check the option 3 in my initial post.

entry.title: Re: Structure ID in TTREE table
entry.content:
The ID here is provided when a new tree is created via the SIMGH Tcode. If you have an existing tree, you can check it as follows:











This ID is also stored in the TTREE table:

entry.title: Get exact percentage score after appraisal
entry.content:
Is there a way to capture the exact percentage score of employees in performance appraisal.

So my client wants to see the overall score in exact percentage after raring. Example: the rating have concluded but it is give a score of 3.78 over 5 ( they are using the 1-5 rating scales) but they prefer to see the 75.6%. Also if a report can give this, it is still fine.

https://help.sap.com/docs/build-work-zone-standard-edition/sap-build-work-zone-standard-edition/expose-html5-applications-in-sap-build-work-zone-standard-edition

entry.title: ID and Description in SAP Datasphere using Business Builder
entry.content:
Hello,

I would like to build a report in the SAP Analytics Cloud based on a data model from SAP Datasphere, which among other things has the dimension "Product". The product should be able to be displayed as an ID and / or as a Description.
Therefore, I have created all the necessary tables, text tables and views in the Data Builder in SAP Datasphere and linked them together. If I now create an Analytic Model in the Data Builder (assigning the semantic relationships of attributes and texts) and use this in SAP Analytics Cloud, I can choose between ID and Description there.
Instead of the Data Builder, I would like to create a Consumption Model with the Business Builder and use it in SAP Analytics Cloud, with which the ID-Description-assignment unfortunately does not work; only the ID is displayed.
I have made the following configurations for this:

  • Attribut "ARTNR" (describes the product) within Fact

     

  • Attribute "ARTNR" cannot be selected in Cosumption Model: There are two attributes "ARTNR.ID" and "ARTNR.TEXT" now ("ARTNR.TEXT" can't be selected)
  • Attribute "ARTNRID" within Cosumption Model

     

  • Preview of "ARTNRID" only shows the IDs

I would like to ask for support.

Thanks in advance!

Best regards,

Marcel

entry.title: Re: Generate multiple invoices from a single sales docume...
entry.content:
Hello @ennouhif 

I assume that you are creating a contract for a period of more than one year and would like to issue an invoice to the customer for each year.

To achieve this, you need to use a quantity contract (WV - Service and Maintenance Contract Type).

If you are using a customized pricing procedure, kindly copy the PPSV condition type and create a new one along with calculation type N (Quantity - Year Price). This ensures that whatever price you enter in the contract for the condition, the system will treat it as a yearly price.

Once you have completed all the necessary configurations, create the contract and enter the contract start and end dates accordingly. In the Billing Plan tab, set the Date Determination Rule as AD and leave the Horizon box empty.

Then, create a billing date. The system will propose billing dates based on the duration of your contract.

Best Regards

Vivek Savant

entry.title: Re: Dynamic Date Range - Set default values in OData v2
entry.content: did u find a way ? have the same issue

entry.title: Re: How to send a Odata Batch Request to SAP Cloud for Customer system using Postman Tool
entry.content:
Hello, 

the following example, I used different changeset to update more than one contact.

--batch_ContactLastName
Content-Type: multipart/mixed; boundary=changeset1

--changeset1
Content-Type: application/http
Content-Transfer-Encoding: binary

PATCH ContactCollection('00163E7A69481ED9A7817FDB2F7A7C04') HTTP/1.1
Content-Type: application/json

{
"LastName":"Postman4"
}

--changeset1--

--batch_ContactLastName
Content-Type: multipart/mixed; boundary=changeset2

--changeset2
Content-Type: application/http
Content-Transfer-Encoding: binary

PATCH ContactCollection('00163E7A69481ED9A8C8BE2DD6105674') HTTP/1.1
Content-Type: application/json

{
"LastName":"Postman 5"
}

--changeset2--

--batch_ContactLastName
Content-Type: multipart/mixed; boundary=changeset3

--changeset3
Content-Type: application/http
Content-Transfer-Encoding: binary

PATCH ContactCollection('00163E7A69481EDA80A8E6C6E81ED452') HTTP/1.1
Content-Type: application/json

{
"LastName":"postman 6"
}

--changeset3--
--batch_ContactLastName--

 

Thanks,
Ravi A

entry.title: Live Sessions in SAP Learning Hub: Interactive Learning with SAP
entry.content:
What are Live Sessions in  SAP Learning Hub? Live Sessions are live virtual events, typically lasting one to two hours, led by SAP experts. They take place throughout the year and cover a wide range of SAP topics. In 2024, more than 200,000 learners took advantage of this offering. To reach as many participants worldwide as possible, the sessions are offered in two time zones and in English.

Live Sessions come in various formats. The majority are:

  • Expert Deep Dives into specific SAP topics.

In addition, there are:

  • What’s New Sessions,
  • Stay Certified Sessions, and
  • Get Started Sessions.

Advantages of Live Sessions in  SAP Learning Hub Live Sessions offer a wide range of benefits:

  1. Expert Knowledge: Up-to-date expert knowledge is shared by SAP professionals, presenting the latest topics and technologies. This ensures that participants are always informed about the latest developments and products.
  2. Interactivity: Participants can ask questions in real time and engage in discussions. This not only promotes understanding of complex topics but also allows for the exchange of experiences and best practices. Learners can also benefit from the questions posed by others.
  3. Practical Relevance: The sessions often focus on real-life use cases. This helps participants apply what they’ve learned directly to their work.
  4. Networking: Participants have the opportunity to network with like-minded individuals from the SAP community. This encourages knowledge sharing and can lead to valuable professional connections.
  5. Flexibility: The sessions are offered at various times to accommodate different time zones and work hours. Some sessions are also recorded
  6. Cost-Effectiveness: For subscribers of SAP Learning Hub, Live Sessions are included in the subscription, providing a cost-effective way to continue learning.
  7. Relevance of Topics: Live session topics reflect the latest trends and developments in the SAP world. Recently, there have been sessions on SAP S/4HANA Cloud, SAP Business Technology Platform, SAP Analytics Cloud, integration of various SAP solutions, and many sessions on Artificial Intelligence.

Live Sessions in SAP Learning Hub complement the on-demand, self-paced learning content perfectly and offer SAP professionals the opportunity to continuously expand their knowledge and stay up-to-date. They are an important building block for professional development in the rapidly changing world of SAP technologies.

 

For those interested, it is recommended to regularly check the offerings in SAP Learning Hub and incorporate relevant Live Sessions into their personal learning plan. Selection filters, such as level (prior knowledge) or role of the learner, help when choosing relevant Live Sessions.
The combination of flexible online learning and interactive live events makes the SAP Learning Hub a comprehensive resource for anyone who wants to stay up-to-date in the SAP world.

Live Sessions are published on learning.sap.com and the schedule is continuously expanded to cover all current product areas. You can find upcoming Live Sessions here.
For regular updates on SAP’s learning offerings, we recommend subscribing to the SAP Training Newsletter.

We look forward to your questions and comments.

entry.title: Re: Advance G/L Account determination issue facing into S...
entry.content:

Your assistance on this matter would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks and looking forward to your response.

entry.title: How do we identify Create/Update/Delete actions in RAP application?
entry.content:
Hi Team,

I have a requirement to enable/disable input fields for input based on Create/Update/Delete action and other conditions in RAP application. How do we identify the current action that we are performing (Create/Delete/Update) in the method "get_instance_features". In core ABAP we do have a field TRTYP = H/V for this purpose. How do we do it in RAP model?

Regards,

Ram Peddireddi.

entry.title: Re: Difference in AP Report and Ledger Reconciliation
entry.content: Dear Experts, We are not running the report on a back date. We are checking as on date.
